title: Time of Troubles
content:
| observedby = 
| affected = 
| activities = 
| first = 
| cause = Theft of the Tablets of Fate
| responsible = Ao
| subevents = 
| outcome = Significant rearrangement of the Faerûnian pantheon
| histrefs = the Time of Troubles was a period during which the deities of Faerûn were forced to walk the earth in their mortal avatar forms. Several major deities died during the Time of Troubles (see Deaths, Ascensions, and Resurrections) and a handful of mortals rose to divinity.

Etymology

Other names that this cataclysmic period was known by included '''the Arrival''', the '''Time of the Avatars''', the '''Fall of the Gods''', and the '''Intervention of Ao'''.) to walk among their followers on Toril.

The immediate effects of this edict were threefold. First, divine magic (spells granted to clerics by their patron deities) ceased to function altogether unless the cleric was within of their deity's avatar. Second, arcane magic (a force channeled from the Weave by wizards and sorcerers) ceased to be regulated by its steward, Mystra, and became dangerously unpredictable.

History

* Ao cast the gods down in the early evening of Kythorn 15. This act unraveled the laws of Realmspace and began the chaotic period of time known as the Era of Upheaval.

Deaths, Ascensions, and Resurrections

Deaths

The Time of Troubles was a time of significant turnover among the gods of the Faerûnian pantheon.
* Eleasis 13: Bane, greater god of tyranny, strife, and hatred, a member of the Dead Three, was slain in a climactic battle with the demigod Torm in battle outside of Tantras. What remained of his essence was absorbed by the Winding Water.
* Gilgeam, demigod and king of Unther, was slain by Tiamat.
* Ibrandul, a lesser god of caverns, was destroyed by Shar, who then masqueraded as the dead deity.
* Moander, demigod of decay, was slain by Finder Wyvernspur.
* Ramman, Untheric lesser god of war and storms, was slain by Hoar by having one of his thunderous lightning attacks repelled back at him. But before Hoar could steal his portfolio, Ramman transferred it to Anhur of the Mulhorandi pantheon.
*The dragon goddess of magic, Kereska, entirely subsumed the worship and portfolio of Kalzareinad, a demigod of dragon magic. Kalzareinad died shortly thereafter.
* Marpenoth 15: Myrkul, greater god of the dead and another of the Dead Three, was killed in a duel with Midnight, a mortal woman wielding the powers of Mystra, in the skies over the city of Waterdeep.

Ascensions

}}
Furthermore, a selection of mortals were chosen by Lord Ao to ascend to the heavens to fill the void left by those deities who died:
* Cyric, a petty, sadistic mercenary, slew Bhaal with the sword ''Godsbane'' (actually the god Mask in disguise). After the end of the Time of Troubles, he was granted control of nearly all the portfolios of the Dead Three by Ao, making him briefly the most powerful of the gods. He then strove to change the horrifying image of death promoted by his predecessors.
* After her death, the goddess Mystra entrusted her essence with the young mage Midnight, who ascended after the Time of Troubles as the new Mystra.

Finally, Lord Ao lifted the barrier that prevented the Mulhorandi god-kings from reuniting with their divine selves on the Outer Planes. The physical incarnations of the Mulhorandi gods departed Faerûn and left governance of the empire to mortal rulers under their guidance.

Resurrections

However, death is anything but permanent in the Realms, and many of these deities did in some way circumvent their own destruction:
* After Bane's death, his portfolio was divided among the newly anointed god Cyric and Bane's own half-demonic son Iyachtu Xvim. Upon the death of one of the greatest forces of evil in existence, all of Faerûn breathed a sigh of relief; however, in 1372 DR, Bane was resurrected, destroying Iyachtu Xvim and reestablishing his church. He also left behind a curse called the Spirit-eater, actually the half-destroyed soul of one of his Chosen.
* Though Ao had decreed that none of the gods fallen during the Time of Troubles should be reinstated, a complex convergence of factors regarding Torm's death led the overgod to make a single exception in his case, resurrecting him and elevating him to the status of lesser god.
* Aerdrie Faenya and Shaundakul initiated a romantic relationship.
* Anhur appeared in Serôs and battled and defeated Sekolah in the Alamber Sea.
* Azuth kept a portion of Mystra's divine essence, stored in a statue of her, safe in the Pool of Yeven.
* The avatars of Clangeddin and Labelas fought on Ruathym, with Labelas winning the conflict.
* Deep Duerra masqueraded as the regent of the duergar city of Underspires, creating an elite army that went on to attack Ironfang Deep and Undraeth, and expanded the city's territory.
* Eilistraee appeared in the High Forest, in the location which became known as Darkmaiden's Leap. The goddess rescued a group of drow refugees and led them to the safety of the temple of the Promenade.
* Eldath spent a lot of time with Mielikki and the two were seen embracing as sisters.
* Garagos slaughtered many people in Westgate's harbor district before wading into the sea.
* Gond washed up on the shores of Lantan in the form of a gnome. His true nature quickly discovered, the Lantanna sheltered and worshiped him until the crisis had passed.
* Gwaeron Windstrom hunted Malar through the North when he appeared there.) with which he armed and mobilized the army of Akanax to attack Cimbar, Soorenar, and Luthcheq. This sparked a greater civil war in Chessenta until they rallied behind Hoar to attack Unther and the church of Ramman.
* Ilsensine appeared in Oryndoll, inspiring mind flayers there to great levels of ingenuity and the creation of the religious Venerator Creed. 
* Lolth briefly appeared in Menzoberranzan, granting Yvonnel Baenre the power to destroy House Oblodra after the crisis.
* Loviatar was assumed by Bane to be in Waterdeep, where he planned to send his chosen emissary, Tempus Blackthorne, to summon her.
* Milil stayed in Arbalest's House, a temple dedicated to him in the city of Athkatla, Amn, and founded a singing circle.
* Nobanion fought and defeated Malar in the Gulthmere Forest.
* Set forged an alliance of convenience with Tiamat after her resurrection.
* Shar appeared in Waterdeep, where she impersonated and imprisoned her twin sister Selûne. The Moonmaiden was eventually freed by her followers and Shar retreated into Undermountain where she attacked and killed Ibrandul. The Dark Goddess later tried to subsume Sharess in Calimport as well but was foiled.
* Shiallia worked to undo the damage Malar had caused during his hunts, forging an alliance with Gwaeron Windstrom.
* Siamorphe was rumored to have manifested in the Assumbar Villa in Waterdeep, protecting many North Ward and Sea Ward buildings from Myrkul's Legion.
* Sseth appeared over the Black Jungles as an enormous winged snake. After battling Ubtao in Chult, He also slew a drow lich named Malyk in the Underdark beneath Castle Crag.
* Tymora's avatar appeared in Suzail, Cormyr, and then traveled to the Lady's House, a temple devoted to her worship in Arabel. She protected that city during the crisis.
* Tyr was blinded by Ao's rage at the theft of the Tablets of Fate.
* Umberlee wandered the seas surrounding the Pirate Isles attacking any pirates she met with storms. She also secretly appeared in the Trackless Sea after foreseeing the return of Iakhovas to make Slarkrethel her seraph.
* Ubtao appeared in Chult in the form of a tyrannosaurus and roamed the jungles. On the eastern edge, he fought with Sseth, the battle ending in a draw.
* Urdlen attacked several rural gnome communities, leaving the curse of lycanthropy in his wake that turned the survivors into weremoles.
* The Red Knight, a little-known servant of Tempus, appeared as an avatar, and possessed the mortal Kaitlin Tindall Bloodhawk. She led a group of adventurers called the Order of the Red Falcon in battle against a horde of monsters in Tethyr. After the war she built the Citadel of Strategic Militancy in honor of her family and the Red knight.

Lasting effects

* Wild and Dead-magic zones, areas where magic behaved erratically or ceased to function, were lasting effects of the Time of Troubles. Some referred to them as "The Legacy of Helm."
* Public opinion on the Church of Helm significantly worsened in the North, owing to the havoc wreaked there. Direct worship of the faith decreased, while persecution of its followers increased.
* Mortals became aware of Ao's existence, leading to the development of many short-lived cults, with the longest-lasting being the Cult of Ao in Waterdeep and their temple Cynosure.
* The stricture that all dwarven priests must be the same gender as their god was relaxed.
* With the Mulhorandi pantheon reunited with their divine essence on the Outer Planes, their mortal incarnations or "lesser avatars" lost their innate divinity, leaving Horustep III as the first truly mortal pharaoh of Mulhorand.
* Some denizens of the Outer Planes theorized that the gods of Realmspace became interested in becoming multi-spheric because of the heavy-handed methods used by Ao during the Time of Troubles.
* The amount of accessible incarnum increased dramatically throughout the Realms.
* The Winding Water became poisoned by the absorbed essence of Bhaal, turning jet-black and foul-smelling. The taint spanned from Boareskyr Bridge to Trollclaw Ford.

Appendix

Appearances

External Links

title: Spellplague
content:
| observedby = 
| affected = 
| activities = 
| first = 
| cause = Death of Mystra
| responsible = Cyric and Shar
| subevents = 
| outcome = Global turmoil, temporary destruction of the Weave
| histrefs = was a disaster that struck Realmspace and even the planes themselves on the date of Tarsakh 29 in the , and was caused by Mystra's assassination at the hands of Cyric and Shar. It continued for a decade, leading to the Wailing Years, during which arcane magic ceased to function and the planet of Toril was transformed.

}}

Omens

}}

In her vision, the diviner Yaphyll referred to Mystra as the white queen and to Shar, goddess of the night, as the black queen. Cyric, god of murder, was the assassin; Savras, the god of divination, was the soothsayer,, and the city, cavern and tree symbolized the ordered structures of magic crumbling into chaos.

Other scholars, however, didn't consider this to be a logical explanation. Historically, two earlier incarnations of Mystra had died before only to be reborn, and the Weave maintained its hold over the power of magic for the duration of her deaths. In these scholars' views, the truth would never be fully known to mortals, and perhaps not even to the gods.

Around 1396 DR, the sentient construct Cynosure theorized that the escape of an entity named the Traitor from its confinement in the dungeons of Stardeep, around the same time Mystra was murdered, may have also influenced the Spellplague's virulent onslaught. Those who came into contact with weaker versions of the Spellplague were sometimes lucky enough to escape with only a spellscar, which could also bestow potent magical abilities. Physical places could also be horribly warped by the Spellplague, becoming a changeland.

The Storm of Blue Fire

 flees from the blue flames of the Spellplague.'']]
The Spellplague manifested in Toril as a storm of blue flames in the Mhair Jungles, west of Halruaa, and quickly grew to a massive size. However, the remaining Halruaan lands in Toril were laid to waste due to the heavy wild magic activity in the area, in such a catastrophic way that the explosion was felt as far as Waterdeep. Sespech, the Golden Plains, and the Nagalands were also affected, and those lands were transformed into an unpredictable and surreal place, dubbed the Plaguewrought Lands. In Abeir, the Spellplague first appeared in Gontal, the southwestern region of the continent of Laerakond, where it made contact with ancient Glaur, the fortress of the Primordial Nehushta. The fortress detonated when the "Blue Breath of Change" ran through it, in an explosion as potent as the Tearfall, rocking the entire planet of Abeir and creating the Glaur Barrens where the old fortress once stood.

That region, where the city-citadel of Djerad Thymar, was located, crashed on Unther, still enveloped in the blue flames. Many believed the Untherites and the Mulhorandi occupation forces were obliterated in the explosion that leveled and reshaped the whole region except for the Black Ash Plain, but actually Unther was instead transported to the world of Abeir to the lands of Shyr, the equivalent continent to Faerûn in Abeir.

The rest of Laerakond was sent to the Trackless Sea, where it was believed it subsumed the lands of Maztica. and Laerakond, which became known as 'Returned Abeir', remained on Toril, in a different location in the Great Sea.

Effects

Effects on magic

* Despite efforts, with the structure of magic out of balance many spells failed or produced unreliable results.
* The Wards of Silverymoon were shattered.

Effects on the Gods

* Savras died as a result of the dissolution of Mystra's home plane, Dweomerheart.
* Cyric was tried by a conclave formed of Lathander, Sune, and Tyr. They found him guilty of Mystra's murder and sentenced him to imprisonment in the Supreme Throne.
* Lathander transformed back into Amaunator.
* Malar was weakened and was said to have been slain by his own beasts.
* Nobanion tried to save a pride of lions in Gulthandor from oncoming blue fire. He came in contact with the blue fire, and it mutated him into a dark and bestial lion with a mane of blue flame.
* Talos was believed to be one of several gods who was severely weakened after the events of the Spellplague, and in an event known as the Stormstar Requiem, he was said to have revealed himself to be the orcish god Gruumsh, which led to his expulsion from the ranks of the Gods of Fury.

Effects on geography

* The Spellplague affected the landscape, making the ground rumble and heave up and down like the surface of the sea. Curtains of blue flame swept the landscape, reshaping the land by cutting crevasses or lifting and sculpting the plain into hills and ridges. Shards of earth wrenched themselves free and became earthmotes. Such areas changed by the Spellplague came to be known as "changelands". Chessenta survived and prospered - though its beloved ruler Tchazzar disappeared without a trace.
* A portion of Shyr violently changed places with large sections of Chondath and western Chessenta. Displaced genasi quickly set about creating a kingdom of their own, Akanûl.
* Chult became a large island when most of the Chultan Peninsula was cut off from the mainland. To save the city of Mezro from destruction, Ubtao's barae created a demiplane and transported their city and all its citizens there. They left behind artificial ruins occupied by undead to discourage people to find what really happened to Mezro. According to the bara Alisanda Rayburton and the naga Saja N'baza, Mezro would not return to Toril as for as long Ras Nsi was alive. Likewise, the Chultan jungle became home to massive monsters from Abeir.
* The Shaar became a wasteland called the Shaar Desolation. In the century since that great disaster, the waters receded, and stories told by travelers from the south told of halfling communities that survived as island redoubts.
* Evermeet was pushed into the plane of Faerie, leaving behind a pale shadow of what the island of the elves used to be, though the isle's residents could move back and forth from Faerie to Faerûn at will.
* The island of Nimbral suddenly and mysteriously disappeared.
* Parts of Conyberry were exchanged with lands from Abeir. The Abeirans survivors were soon accepted by the citizens of Conyberry, and Conyberry became a prosperous village, at least for a time.
* The sudden shifting of continental bodies sent tsunamis all along the island and coastal regions of Faerûn, devastating them all. Lantan was hit harder than most. Before the waves reached the island, all the ''smokepowder'' and magical reagents exploded at the same time, causing great chaos. And as the waves reached the coastline, most of Lantan was engulfed by the blue fire and send to Abeir. What remained of Lantan on Toril was entirely flooded, killing the remaining Lantanese and cleaning away their advanced technology. 

Effects on the planes

* The World Tree was destroyed.
* Most of the Outer Planes were either destroyed or merged with others, creating the Astral dominions. Entirely new Astral dominions also arose because of the Spellplague.
* Dweomerheart itself was dissolved.
* With his newfound godly power, Asmodeus took advantage of the planar instability to hurl the Abyss to the Elemental Chaos in an attempt to end the Blood War. Asmodeus failed, however, as throwing the Abyss into the Chaos did not stop the Blood War, although it was stalled by a hundred years.

Other effects

* Waves of azure fires appeared everywhere, killing whatever they touched. Its energies then reanimated some of those it killed, which arose as hungry undead known as plaguechanged ghouls.
* Humans that were somehow tied to the Shadowfell were affected by the Spellplague and transformed into a "new race", the shadar-kai. 
* The illusions that cloaked the settlements on Selûne failed, revealing them to those on Toril. Some buildings were affected by the aberrant magic, such as Castle Never, the Cloaktower (that was sent to the Far Realm Chunks of earth near and in the Neverwinter's Docks District became earthmotes, including the land on which the Moonstone Mask was built. Also, a small plagueland was formed in the Underdark below Neverwinter.
* In Waterdeep, the Walking Statues of Waterdeep (most resided in the Ethereal Plane) appeared in the city and began to rampage. The Blackstaff at the time was unable to control them or banish them back to the Ethereal Plane.
* The Tower of Twilight, hit by the azure flames, was expelled from the normal timeline and vanished from reality. It did not return to Faerûn until after the eruption of Mount Hotenow, in 1451 DR.
* In Leilon, the monsters that were forced to guard the High Tower of Thalivar and its secrets were released from their bonds to terrorize the city. The magic inside was redirected outward into the town, paralyzing anyone who gazed at it where they stood.

Aftermath

The Spellplague led to political upheaval across the land that resulted in many attempted coups, invasions, and uprisings, especially against governments and nations that had relied heavily on arcane magic for defense or controlling their population.

The number of people gifted with psionics increased exponentially on Toril after the Spellplague. Some scholars believed this was a reaction against the Far Realm-defiling energy of the Spellplague.

By 1479 DR, areas of Toril still affected by the Spellplague were referred to as Plaguelands. In Neverwinter, victims of the Spellplague were expelled from the city to live in Helm's Hold, where those with severe mental or physical afflictions were sequestered in a terrible Sanatorium.

By 1480 DR, the Spellplague had ended with the event known as Mystra's Return. The Weave was resurrected with the goddess and magic became what it was before the Spellplague, though some areas with lingering effects from it remained.

In 1482 DR Lord Ao began the Second Sundering, as a way to restore the worlds of Toril and Abeir of the ravages of the Spellplague.
